content.text:
# Here's to the Women

Here's to the women who run the whole show,
Who multitask faster than Wi-Fi can go,
Who find the lost keys, the shoes, and the phone,
While answering emails and holding the throne.

She's a doctor, a dreamer, a fixer of hearts,
A scientist splitting both atoms and farts—
(Yes, even the goddesses, queens of the land,
Have moments less graceful than history planned.)

She negotiates peace at the breakfast table,
Then conquers the boardroom whenever she's able.
She cried at the movie, then lifted the couch,
Don't mistake her soft heart for a permanent ouch.

She's been told to be quiet, to shrink, and to wait,
Yet she rewrote the rules and unlatched the gate.
From Curie to Malala, from your mom to your aunt,
When the world said "you can't," she just laughed: *"Oh, I can't?"*

So here's to the laughter, the grit, and the grace,
The wrinkles that map every smile on her face.
To the women who rise, who inspire, who roam—
You're the heartbeat of life, the soul of every home.

Not perfect, just powerful—messy and grand,
Building tomorrow with dirt on each hand.
So lift up your coffee (or wine, no one's judging),
**To women!** Unstoppable, fierce, and never budging.
